description: You are a metallurgist analyzing a sample of hot-rolled carbon steel to determine its mechanical properties. Quantify the percentage of the Pearlite phase (dark/textured regions) versus the Ferrite phase (light regions) in the microstructure image located at ~/Fiji_Data/raw/metallurgy/steel_microstructure.jpg. The analysis requires converting the image to 8-bit grayscale, applying a median filter with a 2.0-pixel radius, and segmenting the Pearlite phase using the Otsu auto-threshold method for dark regions. Measure the Area Fraction of the Pearlite phase. Save the binary segmentation mask to ~/Fiji_Data/results/metallurgy/pearlite_mask.png, the measurement results table to ~/Fiji_Data/results/metallurgy/phase_measurements.csv, and a text report to ~/Fiji_Data/results/metallurgy/report.txt containing exactly one line in the format: Pearlite_Fraction: XX.X.
